title: SoCalGas Connects Its First Landfill-Based Renewable Natural Gas Project to Its Pipeline System
content: Southern California Gas Company (SoCalGas) has interconnected its first landfill-based renewable natural gas (RNG) project to its pipeline system at the new WM Simi Valley RNG Facility. WM invested over $100 million in this 30,000-square-foot plant, which captures landfill gas and processes it into pipeline-quality RNG. The facility is expected to generate approximately 2.2 million MMBtu annually, potentially avoiding 100,000 tons of greenhouse gas emissions per yearâ€”equivalent to removing 2,000 trucks from the road. The RNG will be used for heavy-duty CNG vehicles, homes, and businesses. This collaboration supports SoCalGasâ€™s goal to replace 20% of its traditional natural gas delivery with renewable sources by 2030; in 2024, RNG accounted for about 5.5%. Executives from both companies highlighted the project as a critical step in advancing circularity and meeting Californiaâ€™s climate goals.
author: Analysts Account
